stories.title: Wembanyama Returns, Spurs Rally from Down 17 to Beat Blazers 114-93 (Game 4, Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Wemby missed G3 with a concussion. He came back in G4 and put up 27 pts, 11 reb, 7 BLK, 4 stl.

Only the 10th player ever to have that stat line in a playoff game. And the youngest.

Spurs rallied from down 17 to win by 21. #GoSpursGo #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Down 17 at halftime. Won by 21. 

That's the FIRST time in NBA playoff history a team trailed by 15+ at the break and won by 15+.

Fox: 28 pts (11/17 FG), 7 ast, 2 blk. Wemby: 27 pts, 11 reb, 7 BLK.

Spurs lead 3-1. #GoSpursGo #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 9:00 AM ET

Victor Wembanyama didn't just return from a concussion — he made NBA history.

Wemby cleared concussion protocol and started Game 4 against Portland, then put up 27 points, 11 rebounds, 7 blocks, 4 steals and 3 assists in San Antonio's 114-93 victory. That stat line made him the youngest player — and only the 10th — ever to record those numbers in an NBA playoff game.

The story, though, wasn't just about Wemby. The Spurs were down 17 points at halftime, trailing 58-41, and faced elimination pressure on the road. What happened in the second half defied playoff history: San Antonio outscored Portland 73-35 over the final two quarters, completing a comeback that had never been done before. The Spurs became the first team in NBA playoff history to trail by 15 or more at halftime and win by 15 or more.

De'Aaron Fox was right there alongside Wemby — 28 points on 11-of-17 shooting, 6 rebounds, 7 assists, a steal and 2 blocks. That combination had never been accomplished by a guard in franchise history.

San Antonio leads 3-1 and is one win away from the second round. With Wembanyama healthy and Fox at his best, this team is a real threat in the Western Conference.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Wembanyama Returns, Spurs Make NBA Playoff History with Stunning Comeback

stories.title: Celtics Blow Out 76ers 128-96 — Pritchard 32, Tatum 30, Embiid Returns (Game 4, Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Payton Pritchard dropped 32 OFF THE BENCH and the Celtics hit 24 threes in one playoff game.

That's a Boston Celtics FRANCHISE PLAYOFF RECORD.

Tatum 30. Brown 20. Embiid returned and scored 26 — still got blown out by 32.

Celtics lead 3-1. #BleedGreen #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
24 threes in one playoff game. Celtics franchise record.

Pritchard hit 6 of them (career playoff-high 32 pts off bench). Tatum added 30/11 ast.

Embiid came back 17 days post-surgery and the Celtics STILL won by 32.

One win from Round 2. #BleedGreen #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 1:00 PM ET

Joel Embiid came back from his appendectomy after 17 days. He scored 26 points, grabbed 10 rebounds and dished 6 assists in 34 minutes. And it didn't matter at all.

The Boston Celtics absolutely dismantled the 76ers 128-96 in Game 4, setting a franchise playoff record in the process. Boston made 24 of 53 three-point attempts — the most threes in a single playoff game in Celtics history.

Payton Pritchard was the catalyst. The reserve guard erupted for a career playoff-high 32 points, converting six of those record-breaking threes. He had 13 points by the end of the first quarter alone as the Celtics opened the game with a 22-5 run. After that, Boston never led by fewer than 14 points.

Jayson Tatum added 30 points and 11 assists. Jaylen Brown chipped in 20. Three Celtics topped 20 points while their bench player set the franchise record.

Boston leads 3-1 and needs one win to advance. Philadelphia, even with its star player back, has no answer for this Celtics machine right now.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Pritchard's 32, Franchise-Record 24 Threes Fuel Celtics Blowout as Embiid Returns

stories.title: Raptors 93, Cavaliers 89 — CLE 18 Turnovers, Series Tied 2-2 (Game 4, Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Cleveland committed 18 turnovers and lost Game 4 in Toronto 89-93.

Barnes: 23 pts (go-ahead FTs in final minute). Ingram: 23.
Mitchell: 20 (12 in Q4). Harden: 19.

Still not enough. Series tied 2-2. Game 5 in Cleveland. #WeTheNorth #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Barnes and Ingram: 23 each. Raptors win 93-89 in Cleveland's building and tie the series at 2-2.

The Cavs handed them 18 turnovers. Mitchell's 12 Q4 points weren't enough to overcome it.

This series is WIDE open. #WeTheNorth #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 7:00 PM ET

Cleveland thought they had this series in hand. Then came 18 turnovers.

The Toronto Raptors beat the Cavaliers 93-89 in Game 4 to tie the first-round series at 2-2. Scottie Barnes and Brandon Ingram each scored 23 points to power the Raptors, with Barnes hitting the go-ahead free throws in the final minute to seal the victory.

Donovan Mitchell fought hard — he scored 12 of his 20 points in the fourth quarter in a desperate attempt to rescue the Cavaliers. James Harden added 19. But Cleveland's 18 turnovers were the decisive factor. You cannot give away possessions at that rate and expect to beat a hungry team playing with postseason desperation.

The Raptors have now tied the series and stolen home-court advantage from Cleveland. Game 5 heads back to Cleveland, where the Cavaliers will have a chance to reclaim control — but the mental damage of collapsing at home is real.

This series has completely flipped. Both teams are two wins from advancing, with everything on the line.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: 18 Turnovers Doom Cavaliers as Raptors Win 93-89 to Tie Series at 2-2

stories.title: Rockets 115, Lakers 96 — Houston Avoids Sweep, LeBron 10 Points (Game 4, Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
LeBron: 10 pts on 2-of-9 shooting. 8 turnovers.
Ayton: ejected (Flagrant 2) in Q3.
Lakers made just 5 threes.

Rockets won 115-96. Amen Thompson: 23. Eason: 20. Sengun: 19.

Series is 3-1. Game 5 is Wednesday in LA. #Lakeshow #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 9:30 PM ET

LeBron James scored 29 points with a legendary tying three in overtime during Game 3. Sunday was a different story.

LeBron shot just 2-of-9 from the field for 10 points, adding 9 assists and 8 turnovers, as the Houston Rockets dominated the Lakers 115-96 and avoided the sweep. The series is now 3-1 with Game 5 Wednesday in Los Angeles.

Houston did it without Kevin Durant — out with a left ankle sprain — by getting contributions from all five starters. Amen Thompson led the way with 23 points. Tari Eason added 20. Alperen Sengun contributed 19. Reed Sheppard finished with 17. The entire starting unit reached at least 16 points.

The Rockets led 90-65 heading into the fourth quarter and cruised from there. Los Angeles made just five three-pointers — after combining for 35 in the first three games. DeAndre Ayton was ejected for a Flagrant Foul 2 in the third quarter, making a difficult night worse for the Lakers.

Game 5 is Wednesday night in Los Angeles. The Lakers have the series lead, but they need to rediscover their form fast.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Rockets Rout Lakers 115-96 to Avoid Sweep as LeBron Struggles

stories.title: Monday Playoff Preview — OKC Goes for Sweep, DEN on Elimination Brink (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Three massive games tonight:

8 PM — Magic vs. Pistons G4 (ORL leads 2-1) 
9:30 PM — Thunder vs. Suns G4 (OKC leads 3-0 — SWEEP on the line)
10:30 PM — Wolves vs. Nuggets G5 (MIN leads 3-1 — DEN faces elimination)

NBA Monday is not to be missed. #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 9:00 AM ET

Sunday had four playoff games. Monday has three more — and each one carries series-defining stakes.

First up at 8 PM on NBC: Orlando Magic host the Detroit Pistons in Game 4. The Magic lead 2-1 after taking Game 3 on the road, with Paolo Banchero leading the way. Detroit needs a win to avoid falling into an 0-3 hole. This is a pivotal moment in one of the East's most intriguing matchups.

At 9:30 PM on Peacock: Oklahoma City Thunder go for the SWEEP of the Phoenix Suns. OKC leads 3-0. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander has been the best player in these playoffs, and Phoenix faces the final game of their season if they can't win tonight. No team has ever come back from 0-3 in a playoff series.

Closing out the night at 10:30 PM on NBC: the Timberwolves host the defending champion Denver Nuggets in a potential elimination game. Minnesota leads 3-1, though the injury status of Anthony Edwards and Donte DiVincenzo after both left Game 4 hurt is the biggest question heading in. Nikola Jokic will need a heroic performance to force a Game 6.

Three games. Three moments that could define this postseason. Don't miss any of it.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Monday Playoff Preview: OKC Chases Sweep, Denver Faces Elimination, Magic Host Pistons
